tweets.text:
my read of the fable situation:

anthropic decided to take its own customers hostage to improve its negotiating position vs the government

similar to how epic games intentionally broke app store rules in 2020, forcing apple to ban fortnite, affecting 100 million ios players

thread_tweets.text:

thread_tweets.full_text:
my read of the fable situation:

anthropic decided to take its own customers hostage to improve its negotiating position vs the government

thread_tweets.text:
similarly in 2020 epic gradually escalated against apple for months until it finally decided to flat-out defy the most important app store rule, tricking apple's review process and inserting a third-party payment option into the live app

epic knew full well they'll get banned for this, which is why they prepared an entire video ad campaign ahead of time to publicly kickstart their fight against apple

anthropic's recent behavior reads exactly the same way

tweets.text:
I get asked so often how I stay optimistic when the market looks like this for years.

Simple:

I’ve watched this industry for almost 10 years now and it always does the exact same thing. Brutal drawdown, total despair, everyone declares it over, then the quiet recovery nobody believes until it’s already gone.

Sure, valuations might be higher these days, and there are still plenty of structural problems and projects out there that look wildly overpriced.

But at the same time, we have to appreciate that we’ve never had more great onchain businesses with such strong product market fit even beyond the crypto bubble. Institutional interest has never been bigger. Regulatory tailwinds have never been stronger.

Obviously I know just as little as everyone else about what’s coming next, and especially not when.

But one thing I know with deadly certainty:

Not giving up was always the best decision I could make.

Every single time.

tweets.text: Sinclair's new preprint provides the first direct experimental evidence for the Information Theory of Aging: your cells don't age because they break — they age because they forget who they are. The biological blueprint is still intact. The cells just stop reading it correctly. That distinction changes the entire target: you don't repair damage. You restore data. And data can be restored.

The biological blueprint is still intact. The cells just stop reading it correctly. That distinction changes the entire target: you don't repair damage. You restore data. And data can be restored.
